en.extract:
The brown barbet (Caloramphus fuliginosus) is a species of bird in the family Megalaimidae. It is found in Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It is threatened by habitat loss, and has lost approximately half of its connected habitat since 1973.
The sooty barbet was considered conspecific.

The '''brown barbet''' ('''''Caloramphus fuliginosus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Megalaimidae]]. It is found in [[Borneo]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /><ref>{{aut|F. Gill, M. Wright D. & Donsker}} (2015) - [http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ IOC World Bird Names (version 5.2)]</ref> It is threatened by habitat loss, and has lost approximately half of its connected habitat since 1973.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Ocampo-Peñuela |first=Natalia |last2=Garcia-Ulloa |first2=John |last3=Kornecki |first3=Itto |last4=Philipson |first4=Christopher D. |last5=Ghazoul |first5=Jaboury |date=2020-05-05 |title=Impacts of Four Decades of Forest Loss on Vertebrate Functional Habitat on Borneo |journal=Frontiers in Forests and Global Change |volume=3 |doi=10.3389/ffgc.2020.00053 |issn=2624-893X|doi-access=free |hdl=20.500.11850/466694 |hdl-access=free }}</ref>

The [[sooty barbet]] was considered [[conspecific]].<ref>{{aut|Den Tex, R.J. & J.A. Leonarda}}, 2013. A molecular phylogeny of Asian barbets: Speciation and extinction in the tropics, Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution 68(1):1-13. [http://www.consevol.org/pdf/denTex_2013_MolPhylEvol.pdf full text]</ref>
